content: Don't run same service in multiple layers (launchd + tmuxinator)
reason: Causes process pileup and confusion - fixed in orch-go vite management

content: API endpoint handlers share same data types as CLI
reason: ChangelogResult struct already had JSON tags, no new response type needed - enables code reuse

content: MCP only for interactive stateful tools (browser automation, persistent connections). One-shot transformations should use CLI via Bash.
reason: Investigation 2026-01-03 found MCP adds overhead without value for stateless tools

content: changelog detection uses 3-day window
reason: Balance between surfacing recent changes and avoiding noise from older commits

content: Function extraction within package main requires no import changes
reason: All files in cmd/orch share package main, Go handles cross-file visibility automatically

content: status command extraction follows established daemon.go/focus.go pattern
reason: Keeps related code together, no import management needed in package main

content: force flags should not bypass substantive gates only procedural checks
reason: Bug fix orch-go-599i showed --force was bypassing repro verification. Substantive gates require explicit bypass flags with reasons.

content: Hotspot detection uses git history + investigation clustering
reason: Git commit patterns (fix: prefix frequency) combined with kb reflect synthesis detection provides multi-signal hotspot identification without requiring complex AST analysis

content: Hotspot thresholds: 5+ fix commits in 4 weeks OR 3+ investigations on same topic
reason: Conservative thresholds based on dashboard status case study; can be tuned via --threshold flag after gathering real-world data

content: Hotspot indicator uses keyword matching between agent context (beads_id, task, skill) and hotspot file path terms
reason: Simple heuristic that works without needing to track which files agents actually modify - good enough for surfacing architect warnings

content: clean_cmd.go extraction follows established pattern
reason: Consistent with daemon.go, focus.go, complete_cmd.go - keeps related code together in package main

content: God handlers with high cohesion are acceptable if unified around single concept
reason: handleAgents at 451 lines is complex but coherent - splitting would fragment unified flow. Fix is extracting infrastructure, not splitting handler.

content: High patch density in a single area (5+ fix commits, 10+ conditions) signals missing coherent model - spawn architect before more patches
reason: Dashboard status logic accumulated 10+ conditions from incremental patches without stepping back to design

content: Cache infrastructure in separate file
reason: 470+ lines of caching logic (beadsCache, workspaceCache, globalWorkspaceCacheType) is a cohesive unit that belongs in serve_agents_cache.go, separate from HTTP handlers

content: daemon.go extraction should follow existing package patterns (pool.go, completion.go)
reason: Static analysis shows 7 distinct domains with clear boundaries. Package already has proven extraction pattern. Phased approach (P0: zero-deps, P1: adapters, P2: cleanup) minimizes risk.

content: Extract pkg/verify/check.go into synthesis.go and beads.go
reason: check.go is 980 lines with 3 distinct concerns; extraction follows existing domain verifier pattern

content: Svelte 5 bindable props work for parent-child bidirectional binding
reason: Used $bindable for readyQueueExpanded prop in StatsBar component - parent can bind with bind:prop syntax

content: Build verification during serve_agents extraction
result: Blocked by pre-existing daemon package redeclaration issues

content: Check git log before starting work on dependent phases
reason: Parallel agents may complete work before spawned tasks run, causing duplicate effort

content: Phase 2 daemon.go extraction follows Phase 1 pattern
reason: Extracted active_count.go, issue_adapter.go, completion_processing.go using same pattern as Phase 1 (rate_limiter.go, skill_inference.go, issue_queue.go). daemon.go reduced from 1162 to 610 lines.

content: Meta-orchestrator is Dylan's role, documented in orchestrator skill section, not separate skill file
reason: Meta-orchestrator is human role (strategic decisions), not agent skill; skill file implies automation that isn't appropriate

content: Meta-orchestrator requires frame shift, not incremental improvement
reason: Worker→orchestrator was a frame shift (thinking ABOUT workers, not AS workers). Orchestrator→meta-orchestrator is the same. Agents reasoning AS orchestrators can only propose incremental improvements - they can't see outside their own frame. The vantage point above orchestration treats orchestrator sessions as objects to spawn/monitor/complete, just like orchestrators treat workers.

content: skill-type detection via frontmatter for orchestrator spawns
reason: Allows orch spawn to automatically detect orchestrator-type skills and set appropriate defaults (tmux mode) without explicit flags

content: meta-orchestrator spawns use META_ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md with interactive framing
reason: Task-completion framing (Session Goal, work toward goal) causes level collapse. Interactive framing (stay available, no handoff requirement) preserves meta-orchestrator behavior.

content: computeDisplayState in agents.ts computes display state for agent cards
reason: Display state is a domain concern that belongs in the agent store alongside Agent type, not duplicated in individual components

content: copy SESSION_HANDOFF template to workspace with .template.md suffix
reason: using .template.md suffix distinguishes the source template from the agent's actual handoff, making workspace contents clearer

content: Orchestrators don't need beads tracking
reason: Orchestrators are interactive sessions with Dylan, not autonomous tasks. They use SESSION_HANDOFF.md as completion signal instead of Phase: Complete comments. Beads is for work that needs async discovery/tracking - orchestrators don't need that since Dylan is right there.

content: orchestrator completion uses workspace name + SESSION_HANDOFF.md
reason: Orchestrators skip beads tracking entirely, so they need an alternative completion signal

content: Spawned orchestrators wait for orch complete from level above, never run orch session end themselves
reason: Decision 2026-01-04-orchestrator-session-lifecycle establishes hierarchical completion: worker -> orchestrator -> meta-orchestrator -> Dylan

content: meta-orchestrator workspace names use 'meta-' prefix instead of project prefix
reason: Provides visual distinction in tmux windows between meta-orchestrators and regular orchestrators

content: Decision authority criteria documented in .kb/guides/decision-authority.md
reason: Agents had rules but no framework for edge cases - now have six-axis decision tree
